codeReview.summary: Most period-control redesign behavior is implemented: maker-checker period-close request workflow (request/approve/reject), reopen reason requirement with service-level superadmin gate, compliance audit entries for period lock/close/reopen and period-close request lifecycle, superadmin-only period lock enforcement toggle in admin settings, checklist mutation blocked for LOCKED and CLOSED periods, and inclusion of period-close requests in unified approvals dashboard. However, at least one blocking acceptance gap remains in endpoint authorization semantics for reopen relative to the feature contract.

issues.description: Reopen endpoint remains annotated with @PreAuthorize("hasAnyAuthority('ROLE_ADMIN','ROLE_ACCOUNTING','ROLE_SUPER_ADMIN')"), so non-superadmin users can invoke the endpoint and only fail later in service logic. Contract VAL-PERIOD-002 requires endpoint-level restriction to ROLE_SUPER_ADMIN with regular admin/accounting users denied as forbidden at authorization boundary.

AUTH_* error codes are globally mapped to HTTP 401; insufficient-permission outcomes are typically expected as 403. This can blur contract-level differentiation for authorization failures raised via ApplicationException(ErrorCode.AUTH_INSUFFICIENT_PERMISSIONS).

Admin-override compliance audit is implemented for journal reversal overrides only (JOURNAL_REVERSAL_ADMIN_OVERRIDE). The feature narrative says every adminOverride=true usage should be audited; broader create/adjust flows with adminOverride flags currently rely on generic journal creation audit rather than explicit override-tagged compliance entries.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Scrutiny validator should document an explicit manual-review fallback when Task model aliasing is broken, because review subagents may be unavailable despite completed feature scope.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Task calls for scrutiny-feature-reviewer consistently fail in this run with 'Invalid model: custom:CLIProxyAPI-5.3-codex-xhigh-pro'.

summary: FAIL. Core maker-checker and audit redesign landed, but reopen authorization still allows ROLE_ADMIN/ROLE_ACCOUNTING at controller boundary, violating the strict superadmin-only endpoint contract.
